revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:越中の稚児舞]]



'''Echū no Chigo-mai''' (越中の稚児舞) refers to a collection of traditional {{ill|chigo-mai|ja|稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigo-mai|qq=}} (child dancers) performed in Toyama Prefecture, Japan. On January 14, 1982, it was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}. The tradition spans three main districts but is also practiced in two additional regions within the prefecture.
== Echū no Chigo-mai (Designated as Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ==
Performed by four boys of elementary school age, each adorned in white-painted thick makeup and wearing elegant costumes inspired by the Nara period, these dances are characterized by their grace and beauty. The performances are rooted in {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109903880}}, sharing many song titles with it, but their musical arrangements and instrumentation are more similar to {{ill|satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1348260}}. The melodies, played on flutes and drums, are simple yet refined and are also known locally as "Kattondo" and "Ohiyae Ottondo."
* {{ill|Kamo Shrine Chigo-mai|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine Chigo-mai|1=Chigo Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11398889}} - Held at {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimomura Kamo Shrine|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimomura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|qid=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}. The preservation group is Kamo Shrine Ritual Preservation Society (Chigo-mai Division).
* {{ill|Asu no Chigo-mai|ja|明日の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asu no Chigo-mai|1=Ashita no Chigo Mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512132}} - Held at {{ill|Hōfukuji Temple (Kurobe City)|ja|法福寺 (黒部市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfukuji Temple (Kurobe City)|1=Hōfukuji Temple (Kurobe City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119928390}} in {{ill|Unazuki-cho|ja|宇奈月町明日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unazuki-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|en|Kurobe, Toyama|qid=Q753864|lt=Kurobe City|1=Kurobe, Toyama}}. The preservation group is the Asu Chigo-mai Preservation Association.
* {{ill|Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|ja|熊野神社の稚児舞 (富山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|1=Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568967}} - Held at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Fuchū-chō, Toyama City|ja|婦中町中名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū-chō, Toyama City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|qid=Q204266|lt=Toyama City|1=Toyama (city)}}. The preservation group is the Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai Preservation Society.
== Other Chigo-mai in Toyama Prefecture ==
* Chigo-mai of the {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|en|Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|qid=Q11659204|lt=Oyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)}} {{ill|Maedate-shadan|ja|前立社壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maedate-shadan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Held at Oyama Shrine's Maedate-shadan in {{ill|Iwakoji|ja|岩峅寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tateyama, Toyama|en|Tateyama, Toyama|qid=Q670189|lt=Tateyama Town|1=Tateyama, Toyama}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District, Toyama|en|Nakaniikawa District, Toyama|qid=Q1204336|lt=Nakaniikawa District|1=Nakaniikawa District, Toyama}}. It is performed during the autumn festival on November 3. Alongside the Chigo-mai, the "Tateyama no Mai" ({{ill|Miko-mai|ja|巫女舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miko-mai|1=miko mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479841}}) is also offered. On November 27, 1982, it was designated as a cultural property of Tateyama Town.
* Kasuga Shrine Spring Festival Chigo-mai - Held at {{ill|Miyazaki Kasuga Shrine|ja|宮崎鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Miyazaki|en|Miyazaki|qid=Q230660|lt=Miyazaki, Asahi Town|1=Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Asahi Town|ja|朝日町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Town|1=Q1206007|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1206007}}, {{ill|Shimoniikawa District, Toyama|en|Shimoniikawa District, Toyama|qid=Q634439|lt=Shimoniikawa District|1=Shimoniikawa District, Toyama}}. It occurs annually on May 3 and 4 and includes "Spear Dance" (少年の槍おどり) and "Fan Dance" (少女の扇おどり). The latter features girls wearing traditional purple furisode kimonos and floral hats, while boys wear black kimono-style outfits. On September 14, 1971, it was designated as an intangible folk cultural property of Asahi Town.<ref name="Kitanihon20230504">North Japan Newspaper, "Applause for Traditional Chigo-mai at Kasuga Shrine Spring Festival," May 4, 2023, p. 14.</ref>
These five Chigo-mai traditions were collectively included in the "100 Cultural Heritage Selections of Toyama: Festival Edition" in 2006.
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}

== See Also ==

* {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}

== Bibliography ==
* Toyama Festival Guide, North Japan Newspaper Company, March 31, 2004, ISBN 4-906678-87-4.
* 100 Cultural Heritage Selections of Toyama Series (3): Festivals of Toyama, Toyama Prefecture Board of Education, March 2007.
== External Links ==
